heading: Establishment, composition, and vacancies
label: 9101 Establishment, composition, and vacancies
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)9101(a), (b)40:104 (1st sentence).(1st, 2d sentences, last sentence words after comma), .May 17, 1910, ch. 243, § 136 Stat. 3719101(c)40:104 (2d sentence).9101(d)40:104 (last sentence words after comma).In subsection (a), the word “permanent” is omitted as obsolete.In subsection (d), the words “the District of Columbia” are substituted for “Washington” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: There is a Commission of Fine Arts.

children.heading: .—Composition
children.label: b .—Composition
children.text: The Commission is composed of seven well-qualified judges of the fine arts, appointed by the President, who serve for four years each or until their successors are appointed and qualified.

children.heading: .—Vacancies
children.label: c .—Vacancies
children.text: The President shall fill vacancies on the Commission.

children.heading: .—Expenses
children.label: d .—Expenses
children.text: Members of the Commission shall be paid actual expenses in traveling to and from the District of Columbia to attend Commission meetings and while attending those meetings.
